title: SANKALP Firestore Schema
description: NoSQL document blueprint for the SANKALP MVP. Each top-level key is a Firestore collection. Field types use Firestore primitives + GeoPoint + Timestamp + Array + Map.

users.description: Unified profile document keyed by Firebase Auth UID. Citizens, volunteers, and coordinators live in this single collection — separated by `role`. Volunteers carry an additional nested `volunteer` map.

indexes.purpose: Geo radius queries against available volunteers

sos_alerts.description: The heart of SANKALP. One document per citizen-raised SOS. Lifecycle: pending → parsed (Gemini Cloud Function fills `parsed`) → matched (matching engine sets matchedVolunteerUid + matchReason) → in_progress → resolved.

fields.rawVoiceUrl: string | null (Cloud Storage path, ≤30s, max 1MB compressed)

parsed._description: Populated by `onSosCreate` Cloud Function via Gemini 1.5 Pro multimodal.
parsed.summary: string (English, single sentence for coordinator dashboard)

fields.matchReason: string | null (the 'Why you were matched' line)

indexes.purpose: Geo + status queries from volunteer app

missions.description: Volunteer-side mirror of an active match. Created when a volunteer accepts an SOS. Carries route + ETA + on-site state.

fields.routePolyline: string | null (encoded polyline from Routes API)

crisis_zones.description: Aggregated rollup for the coordinator dashboard's predictive heatmap. Updated by scheduled Cloud Function every 60s.

fields.name: string
fields.predictedSurgeMinutes: number | null (e.g. 45 = surge expected in 45 minutes)

securityNotes: Citizens may read/write only their own user doc and the sos_alerts they created.
Volunteers may read sos_alerts within their geohash bucket where status in ('parsed','matched') and matchedVolunteerUid is null OR == self.
Coordinators may read all sos_alerts and crisis_zones; write only matchedVolunteerUid and status transitions.
All write paths involving `parsed` are restricted to Cloud Functions (server-only).
